paragraphs.text: So far, the burden of digital regulations has fallen on consumers: cookies communication is a click window, the customisation of tracking cookies is dozens of entries to be filled in each time the website opens until you agree to it. It is important that users are offered services free of security and that the various cookies or authentication functions of websites are by default disabled. Users who refuse cookies or to hand over their personal data cannot be discriminated against in this respect, each site should also include a version accessible to these persons (although it is understandable that part of the services for which cookies or personal data are needed, e.g. personal location for browsing gps). It is unacceptable that the purchase of digital equipment does not have any kind of warranty of duration or stipulated.’ The consumer should be able to decide which software to upscale without threat on the functioning of their systems (e.g. the firmware of printers, which is regularly updated to maintain the monopoly of the cartridges market) and should have the right to continue using the software that he bought when the manufacturer ends the product support (see the cases of Ubisoft and the revocation of the cd keys of The Crew, or Adobe withdrawing product keys on the older suites regularly purchased to push people into their online subscription services). In-house software has the right to terminate the support as they see fit, but not to render unusable a product regularly purchased by a user; the end-of-life software must always be planned and guaranteed so as to leave the customer what he has paid for. It is absurd that in-house software can change their EULA at any time and introduce costs and payment plans for users and other developers with retroactive effect. See the case of Unity engine and the introduction of its tariff plan in 2023 involving already published video games; Unity removed retroactivity due to backslash, but such practices should be prohibited by law. The privacy of communications must always be guaranteed. Laws such as chat control are characteristic of a fascist state and must remain unacceptable. The intellectual property of your personal data and online productions must be inalienable. It is unacceptable that sites such as Reddit or Facebook have the ability to call their own drawings and texts of their users. Online advertising communications should always be prohibited unless there is a specific request from a customer to send advertising material.
